sections.paragraph: more rarely מָעֹז (with Kametz impure) suff. מָעֻזִּי, מָעוּזּי, pl. מָעֻזּים (from the root עָזַז to be strong), m. a strong or fortified place, a defence, a fortress, Jud. 6:26 Dan 11:7, 10 Dan 11:10, etc. עָרֵי מָעֹוז fortified cities. Isa 17:9 23:4 , מָעֹוז הַיָּם “the fortress of the sea,” i.e. Tyre. Eze 30:15, אֱלֹהֵי מָעֻזִּים “the God of fortresses;” Dan 11:38 used of some Syrian deity obtruded on the Jews, by Antiochus Epiphanes, perhaps Mars. [This entirely turns on the question whether the prophecy relates really to Antiochus Epiphanes.] Figuratively, Psa 60:9, “Ephraim (is) מָעֹוז רֹאשִׁי the defence of my head,” i.e. my helmet. Pro 10:29, “the way of God (is) the defence of the upright,” i.e. religion, piety. Used of a refuge, Isa 25:4 figuratively, of Jehovah, Psa 37:39 43:2 . As to the form מָעֻזְנֶיהָ see the Etym. Ind.
